title: Licensing of Companies Registered with Philippine Overseas Construction Board (POCB)
content:
SECTION 43. Licensing of POCB-Registered Companies. POCB-registered companies with overseas projects may apply in writing for a special license to deploy their workers to their overseas projects, subject to submission of the following requirements: a. Articles of Incorporation; b. Certified true copy of the POCB certificate of registration; c. POCB certification of an existing overseas project; d. Affidavit of Undertaking to submit renewed POCB registration upon expiration; e. Proof of payment of non-refundable filing fee of PhP25,000.

SECTION 44. Payment of Fees. Upon approval, applicant shall: a. Pay license fee of PhP50,000; b. Submit Escrow Deposit Agreement of PhP250,000 with accredited bank authorized by Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas.

SECTION 45. Issuance of Special License. Administration shall issue special license within 24 hours from receipt of application if all requirements are met. Only complete applications deemed filed.

SECTION 46. Validity Period of Special License. Valid for 4 years from issuance date, subject to POCB-registration validity, or until revoked, terminated, suspended, or cancelled by Secretary or authorized representative.

SECTION 47. Requisites for Renewal. Within 3 months prior to expiration, company shall submit written application with: a. Certified copy of valid POCB Certificate of Registration; b. Bank Certificate showing escrow of PhP250,000 intact; c. POCB certification of existing overseas project; d. Proof of payment of license fee of PhP50,000.

SECTION 48. Prohibition on Collection of Fees from Overseas Filipino Workers. POCB-registered companies shall not collect any fee from workers deployed to overseas projects.

SECTION 49. POCB-registered Companies without Overseas Projects. Such companies intending to deploy workers overseas may apply for regular license pursuant to Rule II, Part II of these Rules.
